title: Paratrophis pendulina
taxonomy: Paratrophis pendulina is classified as a species of flowering plant in the family Rubiaceae, commonly known as the madder family. It belongs to the genus Paratrophis. The family Rubiaceae is a large and diverse group of plants that includes many species of shrubs and trees. Paratrophis pendulina is native to tropical regions and is characterized by its pendulous habit and showy flowers. Further classification of Paratrophis pendulina would involve the binomial nomenclature system, with two names that describe the species.

intro: Paratrophis pendulina is a species of flowering plant in the family Rubiaceae, commonly known as the pendulous Paratrophis or Paratrophis tree. The leaves and bark of Paratrophis pendulina have been traditionally used in folk medicine to treat various ailments, including fever, rheumatism, and skin conditions, due to its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which contribute to its potential health benefits.
